headline: Code generation led the quarter as enterprise Claude deployments and multi-agent tooling moved into regulated production.
body: Code generation was the quarter's standout mover (momentum 145, improving), while agentic deployment hardened around compliance: AWS Bedrock now runs Claude Code with data residency, cross-region inference, and prompt caching, and large banks deployed Claude across their organizations. Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit drove long-running and multi-agent collaboration patterns through event-driven, stateless architectures with human-in-the-loop oversight. On the breakout side, privacy techniques rotated forward with encrypted reasoning (ratio 93) and privacy-preserving inference (ratio 112), alongside new entrants in design systems, Content Security Policy, sandboxed iframe, and app marketplace architecture. Diffusion work also advanced, with NVIDIA cutting video generation from 50 denoising steps toward one on Blackwell B200 via quantization, latent chunk caching, and step distillation.
